title: Archived Agent Host sessions should be read-only in editor chat
cleaned_body:
Archived Agent Host sessions are read-only in the Agents window, but editor-window chat surfaces should provide the same behavior.

When an archived session is opened in the editor:

- show an "Archived sessions are read-only" banner
- hide the composer and preserve keyboard focus
- prevent send, retry, fork, follow-up, checkpoint restore, and other mutating actions
- react when archive or chat interactivity state changes

Implemented by [#326455](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/326455).

(Written by Copilot)

title: Slow model download feedback
cleaned_body: I tried to use dictation, and it seems like there was something wrong with the first-run experience. I pressed the keyboard shortcut several times, nothing happened. I clicked the button, nothing happened. Then after ~10 seconds, a progress notification showed up that said it was downloading the model. But the progress bar was full and it never moved. That stayed for a second or two. Then dictation activated. I would suspect that it was downloading the whole time but the progress wasn't triggered until the end. Or we were waiting on a different slow task. If you can tell me how to delete the downloaded model I'd be happy to try again.

title: Dictation immediately fills in odd text
cleaned_body: I thought I filed this before, but I can't find it. I activate dictation, and immediately, before I can even say anything, the preview transcript starts cycling through a bunch of strange phrases like this. Seems like the model is too sensitive silence or something but I hope you can provide that feedback because it's very off-putting, especially if I want to collect my thoughts for a moment before I start talking.

title: Voice mode: manually typed text is dropped when turning on voice mode in a new chat
cleaned_body:
In a new chat, if I type part of my query and paste an image, then turn on voice mode and add to my query via speech, the query is sent but only contains the voice part — the manually typed text is no longer included.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Open a new chat (agents window).
2. Type some text into the input and paste an image.
3. Turn on voice mode.
4. Add to the query by speaking, then send.

Expected: the sent query includes both the manually typed text and the voice-dictated portion.
Actual: the sent query only contains the voice part; the manually typed text is dropped.

Reported by Alexandru Dima.

title: Voice mode: pressing voice mode button while a session is in progress does nothing (spins continuously)
cleaned_body:
Pressing the voice mode button while a session is in progress does not do anything — the button just spins continuously.

Expected: clarify expected behavior when a session is in progress, and either activate voice mode or provide feedback instead of spinning indefinitely.
Actual: continuous spinning with no effect.

Reported by Anthony Kim.

title: NVIDIA Audio Effects SDK does not load
cleaned_body:
Windows 11

_No response_

32.2.0-beta2

_No response_

https://obsproject.com/logs/S99FcVMJrvDyeezB

_No response_

NVIDIA Audio Effects SDK should be properly loaded and enable the NVIDIA audio effect filters.

The NVIDIA Audio Effects SDK fails to load properly.

Notably (maybe?), if you have an older version of the SDK installed, it is found and provides an error saying it is outdated:

After updating from the outdated SDK to 1.6.1.2, it no longer can be found by OBS.

1. Install NVIDIA Audio Effects SDK version 1.6.1.2
2. Run OBS Studio 32.2 Beta2
3. Try to add an NVIDIA Audio Effects filter
4. Observe the filters are missing as the SDK could not be loaded

This may have been caused by https://github.com/obsproject/obs-studio/pull/11569

title: Cancelling a repository clone does not stop the download
cleaned_body:
**The problem**
When I start cloning/downloading a repository with GitHub Desktop and then delete/cancel the download before it finishes, the download continues in the background.

Even though the repository no longer appears to be downloading in the UI, network activity continues and the clone eventually completes in the background.

**Expected behavior**
Cancelling or removing the repository while it is still downloading should immediately stop the clone process and terminate any associated network activity.

**Actual behavior**
The clone process continues in the background even after the download has been cancelled or the repository has been removed from GitHub Desktop.

3.6.2

Windows 11

1. Start cloning a repository.
2. Before the clone finishes, remove/cancel the download.
3. Observe that the download continues in the background.

_No response_

_No response_

_No response_

title: Pull request refresh button is non-responsive when user is not logged in
cleaned_body:
When a user is not logged in, clicking the pull request refresh button produces no visible response. The button is not disabled and no error message is shown — it simply does nothing.

The refresh button should either:
- Be disabled/hidden when no user is logged in, or
- Display an error/prompt indicating that login is required

Note: Whatever behavior should communicate to the user.

The button appears interactive (not disabled) but clicking it has no effect and provides no feedback to the user.

1. Open GitHub Desktop without being logged in to any account
2. Navigate to a pull request view
3. Click the refresh button
4. Observe that nothing happens — no loading indicator, no error, no feedback

title: Desktop crashes on startup for users with Google Antigravity installed
cleaned_body:
We've received multiple reports recently about GitHub Desktop and other applications built on Electron crashing at startup. We're still investigating this but [users have reported that they've been able to resolve this](https://github.com/desktop/desktop/issues/22184#issuecomment-4592111909) by removing a faulty SID (security identifier) from their %LOCALAPPDATA% folder that was seemingly left there by Google Antigravity.

We'll be keeping this issue pinned as we gather more information about this.

See 
https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/51761
https://github.com/desktop/desktop/issues/22184#issuecomment-4578976768
https://www.reddit.com/r/google_antigravity/comments/1tiqpcv/antigravity_20_update_broke_my_electron_apps/

Related issues (may need to be closed and directed here)

- https://github.com/desktop/desktop/issues/22225
- https://github.com/desktop/desktop/issues/22208

title: Selecting a button while ctrl+c allows to copy the query of the button
cleaned_body:
I found out that you can copy the query executed by a button if you select it with your mouse while using ctrl+c. This is not a major issue but still isn't intended behavior.
Signal 8.8.0 on Ubuntu 26. Reproducible at will.

1. G to any chat
2. Click with your mouse
3. Don't release the click and hover above the microphone button near the message bar
4. Press ctrl+c to copy
5. Paste the content

Your clipboard shouldn't have changed

6. you should have in your clipboard: 

    
      document
        .querySelector('.app-loading-screen')
        .addEventListener('dblclick', () => window.showDebugLog());
    

    
    
      window.startApp();

title: Autodelete timer fail on messages transfer
cleaned_body:
On an account which have been set with autosuppress timer, a bunch of message transfered to new contacts (from a group chat) are without autosuppress timer. After consulting with other people, this situation have happened before. It seems specific to messages transfered to new contacts.

1. account set with autosuppression timer
2. previously existing messages
3. a group chat with unknown account
4. transfer message to new contacts

As for any new conversation initied by this account, messages should be automatically timed for autosuppression, as per the parameters set for automatic timer on new conversations.

First message on these new conversations (which are transfered from a previous message) are not timed for autosuppression.

_No response_

title: Audible “hiss” during low-level background audio (since 7.89.0)
cleaned_body:
Since Signal Desktop 7.89.0, an audible “hiss” appears during calls when audio input is very quiet or only low-level background noise is present. It’s unclear if this is exactly when noise reduction engages, but it consistently happens at very low input levels.

1. Start a voice or video call.
2. Stop speaking or speak very quietly.
3. Keep faint background noise present (room noise, mic self-noise).

Quiet input or near-silence should not introduce audible artifacts.

A constant hiss/noise becomes audible during quiet moments.

title: (regression) Microphone access indicator stays on after ending calls
cleaned_body: After ending calls in Signal Desktop, the microphone access indicator appears with the ringing and stays on indefinitely. This started happening about 2 weeks ago. In waybar, the indicator shows that Signal (`Ringrtc`) is still accessing the microphone even after the call has ended. In previous versions, the microphone access would be released immediately when the call ended. **Signal version:** 7.85.0 **OS:** Arch Linux

title: Can't disable auto-updater when it's not supported
cleaned_body:
The function `#checkForUpdates` uses `isNotUpdatable` as a source of truth for whether the signal-desktop is updatable:

https://github.com/signalapp/Signal-Desktop/blob/74a77383aeb7ce584d9e71a798ccb93710d42165/ts/updater/common.main.ts#L555-L560

However, the function `isAutoDownloadUpdatesSupported` performs additional check for the OS:

https://github.com/signalapp/Signal-Desktop/blob/74a77383aeb7ce584d9e71a798ccb93710d42165/ts/types/Settings.std.ts#L65-L73

This means that 3rd-party packages which are not updatable periodically check for updates and it's not possible to disable it.

Install a non-updatable version of Signal.

The auto-updater is disabled or it's possible to disable it.

Auto-updater is still working.

_No response_

7.83.0

Linux

_No response_

title: "Open chat menu" keyboard shortcut no longer works
cleaned_body:
CTRL-SHIFT-L has been the keyboard shortcut to open the chat menu for several years.

Since 7.81, this keyboard shortcut no longer works.

Initially reported in https://community.signalusers.org/t/beta-feedback-for-the-upcoming-desktop-7-82-release/73328/5?u=hiqua and ack-ed by the team, but opening here in case a contributor feels like fixing this.

1. Go to any chat
2. Press CTRL-SHIFT-L

This should open the chat menu.

It doesn't do anything.

_No response_
